note: All links verified from official OEM sources, I-CAR RTS, and OEM1Stop.com

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair Scanning Requirements
statements.summary: All Honda/Acura vehicles (1996+) involved in a collision REQUIRE both pre-repair and post-repair diagnostic scans. Dashboard indicators are NOT acceptable for determining if scans are necessary.
statements.fullText: American Honda's position states that all vehicles involved in a collision (defined as damage that exceeds minor outer panel cosmetic distortion) must have minimum diagnostic scans, inspections and/or calibrations done to avoid improper repair. A pre-repair scan during estimation identifies DTCs present. A post-repair scan confirms no DTCs remain. Any repair requiring disconnection of electrical components requires a post-repair scan. Dashboard indicators are intended for driver notification, not vehicle diagnostics.
keyPoints: Pre-repair scan REQUIRED during estimation phase
Post-repair scan REQUIRED after all repairs complete
Dashboard warning lights are NOT sufficient - scan required regardless
Use i-HDS with 'All DTC Check' feature recommended

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair System Scanning
statements.summary: Toyota/Lexus/Scion requires health check before and after collision repairs to ensure electronic systems operate as designed.
statements.fullText: Toyota recommends that a health check be performed prior to and after any collision repair to ensure all electronic systems are operating as designed. This includes vehicles that may not have visible damage to components containing electronic control units. Access to TIS repair manuals and technical bulletins is available at techinfo.toyota.com. ADAS information is found in Audio/Visual/Telematics, Engine/Hybrid System, Vehicle Interior, and ADAS/AD categories.
keyPoints: Health check BEFORE and AFTER collision repair required
Applies even without visible electronic damage
Access repair manuals at techinfo.toyota.com ($15 for 48 hours)
ADAS calibration required after windshield, bumper, deck lid repairs

statements.title: Pre and Post-Scan of Collision Vehicles
statements.summary: GM REQUIRES all collision vehicles be tested for DTCs during repair estimation AND after repairs are complete to verify faults are repaired and no new faults introduced.
statements.fullText: General Motors takes the position that all vehicles being assessed for collision damage repairs must be tested for Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) during the repair estimation in order to identify the required repairs. Additionally, the vehicle must be retested after all repairs are complete to verify that faults have been repaired and new faults have not been introduced. Many safety and security-related components require calibration when replaced. MDI2 is required for 2021+ vehicles.
keyPoints: Post-repair scan REQUIRED to verify repairs complete
Safety components require calibration when replaced
MDI2 required for 2021+ model year vehicles
Not all scan tools read all DTC codes - use GM approved tools

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair Diagnostic Scanning
statements.summary: ALL Ford vehicles (1996+) involved in a collision REQUIRE both pre-repair and post-repair diagnostic scans for optimal restoration of safety and driver-assist systems.
statements.fullText: For optimal restoration of all safety and driver-assist systems to their pre-accident performance condition, all Ford Motor Co. vehicles (model year 1996 and newer) involved in a collision (defined as damage beyond minor outer-panel cosmetic distortion) are required to undergo both pre-repair and post-repair diagnostic scans. Ford recommends use of IDS (1996-2018) or FDRS (2018+) with VCMII, VCM3 or J2534-compliant interface. Ford does not approve aftermarket, recycled, salvaged, or reconditioned parts.
keyPoints: Pre AND post repair scans REQUIRED on all 1996+ vehicles
VCMII, VCM3, or J2534 interface needed
Follow Ford factory diagnostic and repair procedures

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair System Scanning
statements.summary: FCA/Stellantis requires pre-repair and post-repair scanning on all collision damaged vehicles. MDP with wiTECH 2.0 required for CAN-FD DoIP vehicles.
statements.fullText: FCA/Stellantis has released a position statement requiring pre-repair scan to determine full extent of damage including electronic systems, and post-repair scan to verify all systems functioning properly. ADAS information is located in Chime/Buzzer/Driver Assist, Lamps and Lighting, Speed Control, and Driver Assistance sections of service manuals. The MDP with wiTECH 2.0 is required for diagnostic, programming, and ADAS calibration on newer CAN-FD DoIP vehicles.
keyPoints: MDP with wiTECH 2.0 required for newer vehicles
ADAS info in multiple service manual sections
PROXI alignment may be required on some systems

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair Scanning and Calibrations
statements.summary: Nissan/Infiniti REQUIRES pre-repair and post-repair scanning on all collision vehicles. New position statements released on salvaged/aftermarket ADAS components.
statements.fullText: Nissan/Infiniti requires pre-repair and post-repair diagnosis on all collision vehicles. Mandatory radar calibration prevents malfunctions of Forward Emergency Braking (FEB), Predictive Forward Collision Warning (PFCW), and Intelligent Cruise Control (ICC). Module 'configuration' (programming) is required after replacement. Action tests required after ADAS repairs even when calibration is not needed. Nine new position statements released January 2026 on salvaged/aftermarket ADAS components.
keyPoints: Mandatory radar calibration for FEB, PFCW, ICC systems
Action tests required after ADAS repairs
NEW: Position statements on salvaged/aftermarket ADAS parts

statements.title: Pre and Post-Scan of Collision Vehicles
statements.summary: Subaru REQUIRES pre-repair and post-repair scanning for all 2004+ vehicles involved in collision (damage exceeding minor outer panel cosmetic distortion).
statements.fullText: For Subaru vehicles from model year 2004 and forward involved in a collision, Subaru collision repair procedure requires that pre-repair scanning be performed. Pre-scanning will reveal DTCs for items that are not functioning properly. Subaru defines a collision as 'damage that exceeds minor outer body panel cosmetic distortion.' Post-repair scanning is also required to ensure malfunctioning items have been repaired and appropriate calibrations performed.
keyPoints: Post-repair scan REQUIRED to verify repairs
Collision = damage exceeding minor cosmetic distortion
EyeSight vehicles have additional calibration requirements
Subaru Select Monitor (SSM) required for calibrations

statements.title: Pre and Post Repair Scanning Requirements
statements.summary: Hyundai Motors America REQUIRES pre-repair scan for safe repairs AND post-repair scan to ensure all systems functioning with NO DTCs present, regardless of warning lights.
statements.fullText: Hyundai Motors America requires a pre-repair scan to ensure safe and accurate repairs and also a post-repair scan to ensure all systems and components are functioning, calibrated, and communicating properly with NO diagnostic trouble codes present. Scanning is required regardless if a warning light or malfunction indicator light is illuminated. Kia states scanning 'should not be considered an option, but rather as an essential task.'
keyPoints: Pre-repair scan REQUIRED for safe repairs
Post-repair scan REQUIRED - NO DTCs allowed
Scanning required REGARDLESS of warning lights
Kia: Scanning is ESSENTIAL, not optional
Additional calibrations in Body Electrical System > General Information

statements.title: Collision Repair Diagnostic Requirements
statements.summary: VW/Audi requires comprehensive diagnostic scans and does NOT support use of recycled vehicle parts or assemblies for repairs.
statements.fullText: Volkswagen and Audi require comprehensive diagnostic scans to be performed before and after any collision repair. VWoA does not support the repair of Volkswagen vehicles with recycled vehicle parts or assemblies. Due to the complexity of modern vehicle electronics, collision forces can affect systems throughout the vehicle. Only fastening elements with Dacromet® surface coating should be installed. Wheel repair and reconditioning must meet VW standards.
keyPoints: Wheel repair must meet VW standards

statements.title: Collision Repair Diagnostics
statements.summary: BMW requires pre and post repair diagnostic testing on all collision repairs. Electronic systems are highly integrated.
statements.fullText: BMW requires that pre-repair and post-repair diagnostic testing be performed on all vehicles undergoing collision repair. The vehicle's electronic systems are highly integrated and can be affected by collision damage even when not directly impacted. All repair work must be verified through proper diagnostic procedures before the vehicle is returned to the customer.
keyPoints: Pre and post repair diagnostics REQUIRED

statements.title: Diagnostic Scan Requirements
statements.summary: Mercedes-Benz requires thorough diagnostic evaluation before and after collision repair due to sophisticated interconnected systems.
statements.fullText: Mercedes-Benz requires a thorough diagnostic evaluation be performed before and after any collision repair. Modern Mercedes-Benz vehicles contain sophisticated electronic systems that are interconnected. Collision forces can affect sensors, modules, and wiring throughout the vehicle. Complete system diagnostics ensure all issues are identified and properly addressed.

statements.title: Honda Sensing/AcuraWatch Calibration
statements.summary: Honda Sensing systems often will NOT display DTCs during scan unless there is damage - aiming and/or calibration may be required regardless.
statements.fullText: Honda/Acura noted that 'these driver assistive systems often will not display DTCs during a vehicle scan unless there is damage to the applicable system, so aiming and/or calibration may be required.' Calibration required after: windshield replacement, front radar sensor R&R, front camera R&R, front bumper R&R, wheel alignment, and collision repairs affecting mounting points. Both static and dynamic calibration may be required.
keyPoints: ADAS systems may NOT show DTCs even when calibration needed

statements.title: Toyota Safety Sense Calibration
statements.summary: Cameras need calibration after windshield, bumper, or deck lid R&R. Calibration also required after wheel alignment.
statements.fullText: Calibration ensures that the cameras point in the desired direction. They all need calibration after replacement or after any collision repair that involves removal or adjustment of the body part (windshield, bumper, deck lid, etc) on which the camera is mounted. From a collision repair point of view, that means any time a vehicle has had a bumper cover or grille removed and reinstalled or replaced, or needs a wheel alignment.

statements.title: EyeSight System Calibration
statements.summary: Subaru EyeSight uses DUAL STEREO CAMERAS (no front radar). Calibration required after windshield, camera work, alignment, or suspension work.
statements.fullText: Uniquely in the ADAS market, Subaru vehicles are not equipped with a front radar. Instead, Subaru uses dual stereoscopic cameras mounted several inches apart from the roof behind the windshield. These cameras perform ALL forward-facing ADAS duties. Calibration required after: windshield replacement, EyeSight camera R&R, certain A-pillar/cowl repairs, wheel alignment, suspension component replacement. Tires must be properly inflated and vehicle aligned before calibration.
keyPoints: NO front radar - dual stereo cameras only
Proper tire inflation and alignment BEFORE calibration

statements.title: Ford Co-Pilot360 ADAS Calibration
statements.summary: Ford requires ADAS calibration after windshield, camera, radar, bumper repairs. Static and/or dynamic calibration may be required.
statements.fullText: Ford Co-Pilot360 equipped vehicles require calibration of ADAS components after: windshield replacement, front camera R&R, radar sensor R&R, alignment, and collision repairs affecting sensor mounting or aim. Static and/or dynamic calibration may be required. Ford IDS or FDRS and proper targets are required for static calibration procedures. Ford publishes On Target publications with ADAS guidance.
keyPoints: Proper OEM targets required for static calibration

statements.title: Required Radar Recalibration Post-Repair
statements.summary: Mandatory radar calibration required to prevent malfunction of FEB, PFCW, and ICC systems. Action tests required after ADAS repairs.
statements.fullText: Mandatory calibration is required to help prevent the radar from malfunctioning, especially because it is a main component for Forward Emergency Braking (FEB), Predictive Forward Collision Warning (PFCW), and Intelligent Cruise Control (ICC). After ADAS repairs, action tests must be completed even if calibration is not required. Action tests verify proper operation but do not replace calibrations or diagnosis.
keyPoints: Radar calibration MANDATORY after related repairs
FEB, PFCW, ICC depend on proper radar calibration
Action tests REQUIRED after ADAS repairs
Action tests do NOT replace calibration

statements.title: ADAS Configuration Reference
statements.summary: GM provides ADAS overview documents (Document IDs 5577683 and 5834891) on ACDelco TDS website covering all ADAS components and calibration requirements.
statements.fullText: General Motors provides 'Driver Assistance Systems Configuration Reference' (Document ID #5577683) and 'Important Advanced Driver Assistance Systems Considerations' (Document ID #5834891) on acdelcotds.com. Vehicles with Super Cruise, HD Surround Vision require calibration after windshield, camera, sensor, bumper, grille R&R or wheel alignment. GM approved scan tools and procedures required.
keyPoints: Super Cruise/HD Surround Vision require calibration

statements.title: OEM Repair Procedures Required
statements.summary: ALL major OEMs require collision repairs be performed following manufacturer's published repair procedures. Improper repairs may compromise safety and void warranties.
statements.fullText: All major vehicle manufacturers require that collision repairs be performed following the manufacturer's published repair procedures. This includes proper sectioning locations, welding methods, materials, and finishing. Failure to follow OEM procedures may compromise vehicle safety and could void warranties. OEM1Stop.com and I-CAR RTS provide centralized access to OEM position statements and repair information portals.
keyPoints: OEM procedures REQUIRED for all repairs
Access OEM info at oem1stop.com and rts.i-car.com

statements.title: Tesla Approved Body Shop Program
statements.summary: Tesla REQUIRES repairs by Tesla Approved Body Shops only. Aluminum construction and high-voltage systems require specific training/equipment.
statements.fullText: Tesla requires that all collision repairs be performed by shops enrolled in the Tesla Approved Body Shop program using Tesla-published repair procedures. Due to the aluminum-intensive construction and high-voltage systems, improper repairs can create safety hazards. Tesla provides repair procedures through the Tesla Service platform and requires specific training and equipment.
keyPoints: Tesla repair procedures MUST be followed

statements.title: Vehicle Structure and Unibody Component Replacement
statements.summary: Audi does NOT support aftermarket, alternative, reverse-engineered, or remanufactured parts for structural component repairs.
statements.fullText: Audi position statement covers unibody/structural components including frame rails, apron assemblies, quarter panels, floor structures, bumpers, bumper reinforcement bars, A-pillars, B-pillars, and body panels. These structural components are specifically designed to work together to provide maximum passenger safety in the event of a future collision. Audi does not support the use of aftermarket, alternative, reverse-engineered, or remanufactured collision parts for structural component repairs.
keyPoints: Structural components work together for safety
Frame rails, pillars, floor structures affected
OEM parts required for proper crashworthiness
